text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Solid Tumor — Targeted Panel Sequencing (500-Gene Panel)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Patient Name: Margaret Chen
Date of Birth: [on file]
Sex: Female
Ordering Physician: [Head and Neck Oncology / Otolaryngology]
Indication: HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma, left base of tongue; pre-treatment genomic profiling

Specimen ID: [LAB-OPSCC-2024-00442]
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tumor tissue
Procedure: Direct laryngoscopy with biopsy of left base of tongue lesion
Site of Biopsy: Left base of tongue (primary tumor)
Date of Procedure: [see medical record]
Date Received: [see medical record]
Date Reported: [see medical record]

Tumor Nucleic Acid QC:
  - Tumor purity (estimated by pathology review): approximately 70%
  - DNA yield: adequate for sequencing
  - DNA quality score: pass
  - RNA yield: adequate for fusion analysis

Normal Comparator: Matched germline comparator not submitted; variant filtering performed using population databases (gnomAD, 1000 Genomes) and somatic variant calling algorithms.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
METHODOLOGY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Platform: Targeted next-generation sequencing, 500-gene panel (Illumina NovaSeq)
Sequencing Coverage: Mean on-target depth: 742x (tumor); variant calling threshold: minimum 5% VAF at ≥100x coverage
Analysis: Detection of somatic single-nucleotide variants (SNVs), insertions/deletions (indels), copy number alterations (CNAs), and gene fusions/rearrangements. Microsatellite instability (MSI) assessed using 114 microsatellite loci. Tumor mutational burden (TMB) calculated from somatic non-synonymous coding mutations per megabase (Mb) across the coding regions of the panel. PD-L1 combined positive score (CPS) reported from concurrent IHC performed on the same specimen block (22C3 antibody, Dako).
Limitations: This assay detects somatic alterations in the targeted gene regions only. Deep intronic variants, low-level mosaicism below detection threshold, and balanced translocations not in the target panel may not be detected. Results should be interpreted in the context of clinical and pathologic findings.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENTARY ON TUMOR HISTOLOGY AND MOLECULAR CONTEXT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This specimen is derived from a confirmed HPV-positive (p16 strongly positive by IHC; HPV-16 confirmed by ISH) oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma (OPSCC), moderately differentiated. HPV-positive OPSCC has a distinct mutational landscape characterized by TP53 wild-type status, enrichment of PIK3CA hotspot mutations, and frequent alterations in TRAF3, KMT2D, NOTCH1, and EP300. These features are reflected in the current specimen.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SOFTWARE AND REFERENCE DATABASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Genome reference: GRCh38/hg38
Variant annotation: OncoKB (v3.19), ClinVar, COSMIC (v98), dbSNP (v155), gnomAD (v3.1.2)
Fusion detection: STAR-Fusion, arriba
CNA analysis: CNVkit (segmentation and ploidy correction)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GENOMIC FINDINGS — SOMATIC MUTATION SUMMARY TABLE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gene        | Alteration                         | Exon  | VAF   | Copy Number | Classification
------------|------------------------------------|-------|-------|-------------|------------------------------
PIK3CA      | p.E545K (c.1633G>A)                | 9     | 28%   | Gain (3q26) | Likely Oncogenic — Tier I
TRAF3       | p.R242* (c.724C>T; truncating)     | 7     | 41%   | Neutral     | Likely Pathogenic — Tier II
KMT2D       | p.Q1555* (c.4663C>T; truncating)   | 31    | 35%   | Neutral     | Loss of Function — Tier II
EP300       | p.S1415fs*14 (frameshift del)      | 22    | 19%   | Neutral     | Loss of Function — Tier II
FBXW7       | p.R465C (c.1393C>T)               | 8     | 22%   | Neutral     | Likely Oncogenic — Tier II
FAT1        | p.E2386* (c.7156G>T; truncating)   | 27    | 31%   | Neutral     | Likely Loss of Function — Tier III
NOTCH1      | p.R2382* (c.7144C>T; truncating)   | 34    | 18%   | Neutral     | Loss of Function (Tumor Suppressor) — Tier II
TP53        | No pathogenic alteration detected  | —     | —     | —           | Wild-type (expected in HPV+ OPSCC)
KRAS        | No pathogenic alteration detected  | —     | —     | —           | Wild-type
EGFR        | No pathogenic alteration detected  | —     | —     | —           | Wild-type; no amplification
CDKN2A      | No pathogenic alteration detected  | —     | —     | —           | Wild-type
FGFR1-4     | No pathogenic alteration detected  | —     | —     | —           | Wild-type
CCND1       | No amplification detected          | —     | —     | Neutral     | Wild-type
MYC         | No amplification detected          | —     | —     | Neutral     | Wild-type
AKT1        | No pathogenic alteration detected  | —     | —     | —           | Wild-type
MTOR        | No pathogenic alteration detected  | —     | —     | —           | Wild-type

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - PIK3CA (3q26.32): Low-level copy gain (estimated 4-5 copies). Not high-level amplification. May contribute to PI3K pathway activation in conjunction with the p.E545K hotspot mutation.
  - No high-level amplifications (>= 6 copies) detected in any gene on the panel.
  - No homozygous deletions detected.
  - Global copy number profile: low aneuploidy; consistent with HPV-positive OPSCC (which typically has a relatively stable karyotype compared to HPV-negative HNSCC).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FUSIONS / STRUCTURAL REARRANGEMENTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  No clinically actionable gene fusions or rearrangements detected.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BIOMARKER SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):
  7.2 mutations/Mb — INTERMEDIATE
  (Reference: TMB-low <6 mut/Mb; TMB-intermediate 6–19 mut/Mb; TMB-high ≥20 mut/Mb)
  Note: HPV-positive OPSCC characteristically exhibits intermediate TMB; viral oncoproteins (E6/E7) drive carcinogenesis rather than tobacco-associated mutagenesis, consistent with this patient's never-smoker status.

Microsatellite Instability (MSI):
  MSI-Stable (MSS)
  (0/114 informative loci show instability)

PD-L1 Expression (CPS, 22C3):
  CPS: 18 (POSITIVE)
  (CPS ≥1 is considered positive; CPS ≥20 is a higher expression category for pembro indications in HNSCC)
  Note: CPS of 18 falls below the ≥20 threshold for first-line Keytruda monotherapy approval in R/M HNSCC per KEYNOTE-048; however, all patients with CPS ≥1 derived benefit from pembro-containing regimens in that trial.

HPV Status (IHC/ISH, from diagnostic pathology — concordant with molecular profile):
  p16: Diffuse strong positive (>70% of tumor cells)
  HPV-16 ISH: Positive (confirmed high-risk HPV type 16)
  Molecular sequencing: HPV-16 reads identified in tumor specimen (integrated signal); no HPV-18 or other high-risk type detected.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERPRETATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The somatic mutational profile of this HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma is consistent with published genomic characterizations of this disease subtype (TCGA HNSCC cohort; Gillison et al.). Key findings and their significance are as follows:

1. PIK3CA p.E545K (Exon 9 Hotspot Mutation) + PIK3CA Low-Level Copy Gain:
   This is a well-characterized oncogenic hotspot mutation in the helical domain of PIK3CA, encoding the p110-alpha catalytic subunit of PI3-kinase. The p.E545K variant constitutively activates downstream AKT/mTOR signaling. PIK3CA mutations occur in approximately 20-35% of HPV-positive OPSCCs and are among the most common oncogenic driver events in this setting. Concurrent low-level copy gain at 3q26 may further enhance PI3K pathway output. This finding is of potential clinical relevance as PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway inhibitors (e.g., alpelisib, copanlisib) are under investigation in head and neck cancers harboring PIK3CA alterations.

2. TRAF3 p.R242* (Truncating Mutation, VAF 41%):
   TRAF3 encodes a negative regulator of the NF-kB and type I interferon pathways. Truncating mutations in TRAF3 are enriched in HPV-positive HNSCC (occurring in ~10-17% of cases) and are thought to facilitate viral immune evasion and NF-kB pathway activation. The high VAF (41%) suggests this may be a clonal, early event. TRAF3 alterations are not directly actionable with approved therapies but provide context for immune microenvironment biology.

3. KMT2D p.Q1555* (Truncating Mutation, VAF 35%):
   KMT2D (also known as MLL4) is a histone H3K4 methyltransferase with tumor suppressor function. Loss-of-function mutations are among the most frequent epigenetic alterations across many cancers, including HNSCC. KMT2D loss can impair chromatin remodeling and transcriptional regulation. No approved targeted therapies directly target KMT2D loss, though preclinical data suggest sensitivity to certain HDAC inhibitors.

4. EP300 p.S1415fs*14 (Frameshift Deletion, VAF 19%):
   EP300 encodes the transcriptional coactivator p300, a histone acetyltransferase. Frameshift mutations result in loss of function. EP300 mutations co-occur with KMT2D alterations in HNSCC and together reflect a pattern of epigenetic dysregulation. The relatively lower VAF (19%) may indicate a subclonal event.

5. FBXW7 p.R465C (VAF 22%):
   FBXW7 is an E3 ubiquitin ligase substrate receptor that targets oncoproteins including Cyclin E, c-Myc, Notch, and c-Jun for proteasomal degradation. The p.R465C variant occurs at one of the three canonical hotspot arginine residues (R465, R479, R505) in the WD40 substrate-binding domain, impairing ubiquitin-mediated degradation of oncogenic substrates. Not directly actionable with approved agents.

6. FAT1 p.E2386* (Truncating Mutation, VAF 31%):
   FAT1 is a protocadherin with tumor suppressor function that modulates Wnt/beta-catenin and Hippo/YAP signaling. FAT1 inactivating mutations are found in approximately 15-25% of HNSCC. Loss of FAT1 may promote YAP nuclear translocation and oncogenic transcriptional programs. Preclinical data suggest potential sensitivity to YAP/TEAD inhibitors, none of which are currently approved.

7. NOTCH1 p.R2382* (Truncating Mutation, VAF 18%):
   NOTCH1 truncating mutations in HNSCC are thought to represent loss of tumor suppressor function (distinct from activating NOTCH1 mutations seen in T-ALL). Loss of NOTCH1-mediated differentiation signaling may contribute to squamous cell carcinoma progression. No approved targeted therapies.

8. TP53 Wild-Type:
   The absence of TP53 mutation is consistent with HPV-positive OPSCC, in which HPV E6 oncoprotein mediates p53 degradation via the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway. TP53 mutations are hallmarks of HPV-negative, tobacco-associated HNSCC but are uncommon in HPV-positive disease.

9. KRAS, EGFR, FGFR, CCND1 — Wild-Type:
   No alterations in these commonly targeted genes were detected. Absence of EGFR amplification is expected in the HPV-positive subtype. KRAS mutation would be highly atypical in this histologic and etiologic context.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ACTIONABILITY TIERS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Tier I — Alterations with established clinical significance / FDA-approved therapies or companion diagnostic implications:

  PIK3CA p.E545K
    Alteration: Activating hotspot mutation, helical domain
    Potential significance: PI3K pathway activation; investigational PI3K/AKT/mTOR inhibitors (e.g., alpelisib, copanlisib, gedatolisib) are being evaluated in PIK3CA-altered HNSCC in clinical trials. No FDA-approved PI3K inhibitor indication exists specifically for HNSCC at this time; however, this finding may be relevant for enrollment in basket trials or PIK3CA-selected studies. Additionally, may influence response to de-escalation strategies (some data suggest PIK3CA mutation is associated with slightly higher recurrence risk in HPV-positive OPSCC).
    Standard-of-care implication: Identification of PIK3CA mutation noted as potentially relevant to risk stratification in HPV-positive OPSCC de-escalation trial eligibility.

  PD-L1 CPS 18 (Positive, <20)
    Potential significance: In the recurrent/metastatic HNSCC setting, Keytruda (Keytruda) monotherapy is FDA-approved for PD-L1 CPS ≥1 tumors, and pembro + platinum/5-FU is approved for CPS ≥1. CPS 18 confirms PD-L1 positivity. This is relevant if disease were to recur in a metastatic setting. In the current curative-intent setting, PD-L1 status does not change standard management.

  MSI/TMB:
    MSI-Stable; TMB intermediate (7.2 mut/Mb). TMB-high (≥10 mut/Mb) threshold required for tumor-agnostic Keytruda approval (KEYNOTE-158) is not met. MSS status also does not support checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy on a biomarker-only basis.

Tier II — Alterations with potential clinical significance; evidence from clinical trials, case series, or strong preclinical data:

  TRAF3 p.R242* — Loss of negative NF-kB regulation; no approved agent; research context only
  KMT2D p.Q1555* — Epigenetic loss of function; potential preclinical rationale for HDAC inhibitor sensitivity
  EP300 p.S1415fs*14 — Histone acetyltransferase loss; epigenetic dysregulation
  FBXW7 p.R465C — Impaired oncoprotein degradation; loss of tumor suppressor function
  NOTCH1 p.R2382* — Loss of differentiation signaling; tumor suppressor context

Tier III — Alterations of uncertain or limited clinical significance:

  FAT1 p.E2386* — Tumor suppressor loss, Hippo/Wnt pathway; not yet actionable with approved agents

Tier IV — Benign / likely benign variants:
  None reported in this summary.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL GENOMICS SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma demonstrates a mutational landscape consistent with the HPV-positive HNSCC molecular subtype: TP53 wild-type, PIK3CA helical domain hotspot mutation, and co-occurring truncating mutations in TRAF3, KMT2D, EP300, FBXW7, FAT1, and NOTCH1. The most immediately clinically relevant finding is the PIK3CA p.E545K activating mutation, which activates the PI3K/AKT/mTOR axis and may have implications for trial eligibility or risk stratification in the HPV-positive OPSCC setting. PD-L1 CPS of 18 confirms immunologic expression relevant to checkpoint inhibitor use in the recurrent/metastatic setting should that circumstance arise. TMB is intermediate and MSI is stable.

Results should be correlated with clinical, radiologic, and pathologic findings. This report does not constitute a treatment recommendation. Clinical interpretation and management decisions remain the responsibility of the treating physician.

Report Verified By: [Molecular Pathologist / Clinical Genomics Laboratory Director]
CAP/CLIA Accredited Laboratory
